message:
refactor(mvcc): return post-enforcement chain_len from enforce_chain_bound_for_page

TransactionManager::enforce_chain_bound_for_page was:
  fn enforce_chain_bound_for_page(&self, pgno) -> Result<(), MvccError>

Inside, it called version_store.chain_length(pgno), made its
threshold/cleanup decisions against that value, and returned Ok(()).
Every caller that needed the post-enforcement length immediately
called chain_length(pgno) again — a full traversal of the freshly-
adjusted version chain, redundantly.

New shape:
  fn enforce_chain_bound_for_page(&self, pgno) -> Result<usize, MvccError>

The three Ok(()) returns (below-threshold, after opportunistic cleanup,
and soft-bound-exceeded-proceeding) now return Ok(chain_len) with the
length the enforcement routine already had in hand. Hard-limit Busy
return is unchanged.

No change in enforcement policy, no change in error paths; just stops
duplicate chain walks at every call site.
